crime.value: Double murder within the Greene family; gunshot death of at least one family member; central question involves the mechanism and psychology of the crime, with suggestion of extraordinary technical ingenuity beyond ordinary investigation.
crime.quote: A Double Tragedy...one of the outstanding murder mysteries of modern times—a case practically unique in the annals of latter-day crime.

victim.value: At minimum, one Greene family member (likely Chester or Rex Greene based on narrative focus); victim killable due to position within wealthy household with complex family dynamics and secrets.
victim.quote: your son is dead...the tragedy is causing you

closed_world.value: The Greene mansion in New York County; bounded household with staff (butler, cook, maids, nurse, physician), family members, and limited external access during the critical timeframe.
closed_world.quote: the Greene mansion...Characters of the Book [listing household and family members]

resolution.value: Resolution achieved through analytical deduction by Philo Vance using novel methods of criminal deduction beyond ordinary police investigative procedures; involves understanding the 'warped psychological motives' and 'hidden sources of technic' underlying the crime.
resolution.quote: The man who elucidated the mystery...had it not been for him and his novel methods of criminal deduction, the heinous plot...would have been conclusively successful.

investigator.value: Philo Vance, a young social aristocrat with no official police connection; intimate friend of District Attorney John F.-X. Markham; operates as amateur detective applying superior analytical methods.
investigator.quote: Philo Vance...a young social aristocrat, an intimate friend of John F.-X. Markham, the District Attorney...no way ofﬁcially connected with the police
